patch.summary: Map Role/Create Category to Group Tree: Adds a role/category to the roles group tree

parameters.name: account_type
parameters.description: account_type/group_id to fetch: 1=Vestas account; 2=Service account; 3=Third Party account; 4=Customer account

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: *should be an existing role in Ldap

parameters.name: category_name
parameters.description: *for adding a category, the role should be blank

parameters.name: parent
parameters.description: *optional; an item in the tree where the new role/category will be placed under; default=root

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: *optional; an item in the tree where the new role/category will be placed under; default=root

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: *optional; an item with the same parent where the new role/category will be placed before; if both after and before are not set, default location is to the last of the list under the same parent

message.example: Map role to user group successful.
Map role to user group failed.

error.example: Position reference(Test Dummy Role B) does not exist in Account Group(4).

parameters.description: *required; for Service account, this is the owner's last name

parameters.name: mobile_number
parameters.description: *required for Customer and Third Party account; must be a valid number

parameters.name: account_expiry_utc

parameters.name: owner_username
parameters.description: *optional; for Service account only; username of the service account's owner; must be an existing ldap username

get.summary: Get All Users: Gets the entire list of users(or just a set) including their complete details

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: The amount of users you are willing to receive per API call (default: gets all)

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A piece of code to tell the API from what user it should continue getting the list of users and their details. This is provided by the API's response after the first fetch in 'info.next_url'. If the result reached the last user, no next_url will be provided. This is advisable method for fetching sets as it is not affected by sudden addition or deletion of users in LDAP. (overrides page, get_start and get_end; default: start from beginning unless get_start or page is set.)

parameters.name: api_url
parameters.description: An option to specify the prepended URL in the result's 'next_url'. (default: Uses the current URL of the API)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies from where the next set will be fetched where the per page is multiplied by pagesize e.g. when page=2 and pagesize=10, gets users from index 20-29. (default: 0 if get_start and after are not set)

parameters.name: get_start
parameters.description: A more specific way to tell from where to start fetching the set of users based on their index. Note that this may have redundant or skipped user if there were sudden addition or deletion of user in LDAP in between the API calls. (default: 0 if page and after are not set.)

parameters.name: get_end
parameters.description: A more specific way to tell until where the API should fetch the set of users based on their index. (overrides the pagesize; default: 500 unless pagesize is set)
